title: branch — compliance evidence
description: Branch carries no PII directly (the address is business location, the phone / email are business contacts). Compliance responsibilities concentrate around three axes: (a) every branch state change (create/close/reopen/pause) is compliance-audited under SOC 2 CC6.3, (b) closure cascades to notify staff — a subject notification requirement under GDPR + regional labor law, (c) financial regulation (many jurisdictions require historic invoice records for 7 years, and invoices reference branch_id snapshot — Branch rows can never be silently disappeared).

controls.evidence: Notifying staff on branch closure IS contract performance — the employment relationship gives the tenant a legal obligation to inform. Closure notifications are `transactional_required` (no opt-out) for exactly this reason.
See subprocessors.json. DPAs signed with Google Maps, Mapbox, Nominatim / OSM Foundation. Tenant-level opt-in for geocoding via the `branch_geocoding` entitlement toggle.

Every Branch state change (create / update / pause / resume / close / reopen / address change / coordinate change / hours change / capacity change) is Auditable. Audit rows carry actor + timestamp + before/after diff + request_id.
Geocoding subprocessors offer EU-region processing (Google Maps `region=EU`, Nominatim EU-based by default). Tenants in EU regions can pin the subprocessor to EU via `branch.geocoding.provider`.

coppa.note: Branch itself carries no minor data. The `is_publicly_listed` toggle (deferred beyond Wave 2) would need COPPA review if enabled — a public directory that lists a branch operating a youth-athletics program raises secondary-audience concerns. Not a Wave 2 concern.

controls.evidence: Every write path is behind a BranchPolicy check. Close + reopen are owner-tier only. Cross-tenant reads on the platform surface require `platform.branches.viewAny`. Force-close bypasses the dependency check + fires an alert on every use.

Every Branch state change writes to `audits`. Audit rows OUTLIVE Branch rows — a hard-deleted Branch still shows up in audits keyed by branch_id ULID. `retention.json` documents the 90d / 1yr / 7yr hot windows per tier.

Geocoding subprocessor calls TLS 1.2+. API keys stored via env vars, never in DB. The Branch ULID is opaque metadata — reverse-linkage to tenant identity is impossible from the geocoding payload alone.

Health probes cover FK integrity (Organization + Region + Tenant), default-branch invariant integrity, geocoder reachability, stuck-geocoding-job detection. `stackra.branch.reconcile.corrections` metric alerts on drift.

controls.regulation: generic 7-year invoice retention (US GAAP, EU accounting directives, GCC VAT law)
controls.evidence: Invoices generated by the finance module (Wave 4) snapshot `branch_id + region_id + branch.name + branch.address.formatted` at issue time. Hard-deleting a Branch would corrupt those historic invoices — which is why hard-delete is a two-person platform-support action, never a public API path, and why `status=closed` is preserved indefinitely.

controls.jurisdiction: regional (WARN Act in the US, TUPE in the UK, etc.)
controls.evidence: The `branch.settings.closure_notice_days` setting clamps up to the region's regulatory minimum (60 days in the US for large employers under WARN; region-specific for EU). Clamping happens in the compliance-service at settings read time — tenants cannot set a value below their region's floor.

Branch.capacity + the `branch_capacity_gating` entitlement enable the fire-safety cap on booking-adjacent write paths. When on, the platform refuses new bookings that would exceed capacity. Every capacity change is audit-logged for compliance inspection.

The `amenities` array includes `wheelchair_accessible` in the platform catalogue. Tenants marking a branch accessible without meeting ADA / regional equivalent standards expose themselves to a compliance claim; the platform ships a per-tenant setting to gate this claim behind a documentation link.
